back_translation: On the twenty-fourth day of the same month, the Israelites gathered together with fasting and sackcloth, with dirt upon them.
The seed of Israel separated themselves from all the foreigners, and they stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.
While they stood in their places, they read from the Book of the Law of the LORD their God for a quarter of the day, and for another quarter they confessed and worshiped the LORD their God.
Jeshua, Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, and Chenani — who were Levites — stood on the raised platform and cried out to the LORD their God with a loud voice.

Then Jeshua, Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabneiah, Sherebiah, Hodiah, Shebaniah, and Pethahiah — the Levites — said, "Stand up and bless the LORD your God from everlasting to everlasting. May Your glorious name be blessed, and may it be exalted above all blessing and praise.

You Yourself are the LORD alone. You made the heavens, the heaven of heavens with all their host, the earth and all that is on it, the seas and all that is in them. You give life to all of them, and the host of heaven worships You.
You Yourself are the LORD, the God who chose Abram, who brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans and gave him the name Abraham.

You found his heart faithful before Your presence and made a covenant with him to give the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Jebusites, and Girgashites — to give it to his descendants. And You have fulfilled Your word, because You are righteous.
You saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt and heard their cry at the Red Sea.

You performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh, all his officials, and all the people of his land, because You knew they had acted arrogantly against our fathers. You made Your name known to this day.
You divided the sea before them, and they crossed through the sea on dry ground. You threw their pursuers into the depths like a stone falling into raging waters.
You led them with a pillar of cloud by day, and with a pillar of fire by night, to illuminate the way they should travel.
You came down on Mount Sinai, You spoke to them from heaven, and You gave them right ordinances, true laws, good statutes and commandments.
You revealed Your holy Sabbath to them and commanded commandments, statutes, and law to them through Moses Your servant.

In their hunger, You gave them bread from heaven; in their thirst, You brought water from the rock for them; and You commanded them to enter and possess the land that You had sworn-with-uplifted-hand to give them.
But they and our fathers became arrogant, made their necks stiff, and did not listen to Your commandments.

They refused to listen and did not remember the wonders You performed among them. They stiffened their necks and appointed a leader to return to their bondage. But You are a forgiving God, gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love. You did not abandon them.
Even when they made for themselves a molten calf and said, 'This is your god who brought you up from Egypt,' and committed great blasphemies,

You in Your great compassion did not abandon them in the wilderness. The pillar of cloud did not depart from them by day to guide them on the path, and the pillar of fire by night to illuminate for them the way they should go.
You gave Your good Spirit to teach them. You did not withhold Your manna from their mouths, and You gave them water for their thirst.
For forty years You sustained them in the wilderness, so they lacked nothing. Their clothing did not wear out, and their feet did not swell.
You gave them kingdoms and peoples and apportioned them throughout every corner of the land. So they took possession of the land of Sihon, the king of Heshbon, and the land of Og king of Bashan.
You multiplied their descendants like the stars of heaven, and brought them into the land that You had told their fathers to enter and possess.

So the descendants went in and possessed the land. You subdued the Canaanite inhabitants of the land before them, and gave the kings and peoples of the land into their hand, to do with them as they wished.

They captured fortified cities and fertile land. They took possession of houses full of every good thing, wells that had been dug, vineyards, olive groves, and fruit trees in abundance. They ate, were filled, became fat, and delighted in Your great goodness.

But they were disobedient and rebelled against You. They threw Your law behind their backs and killed Your prophets who had testified against them to turn them back to You. They committed great blasphemies.

So You delivered them into the hand of enemies who oppressed them. In their time of distress they cried out to You, and You heard from heaven. In Your great compassion You gave them deliverers, who saved them from the hand of their enemies.

But as soon as they had rest, they returned to do evil before Your presence again. So You abandoned them into the hand of their enemies, who ruled over them. When they cried out to You again, You heard from heaven and delivered them in Your compassion many times.

You testified against them to return them to Your law, but they were arrogant and did not listen to Your commandments. They sinned against Your ordinances, by which a person practicing them shall live. They turned a stubborn shoulder, stiffened their necks, and would not listen.
You bore patiently with them for many years and testified against them by Your Spirit through Your prophets, but they would not listen. So You gave them into the hand of the peoples of the lands.
But in Your great compassion You did not bring them to an end, nor did You abandon them, because You are a gracious and compassionate God.

And now, our God, the great, mighty, and awesome God, who keeps the covenant and the steadfast love, do not regard as small all the hardship that has come upon us — upon our kings, our leaders, our priests, our prophets, our fathers, and all Your people — from the days of the kings of Assyria until today.
You are righteous in all that has come upon us, because You have acted in truth, while we have acted wickedly.
Our kings, our leaders, our priests, and our fathers did not follow Your law, and did not pay attention to Your commandments and Your warnings that You gave them.

Even when they were in their kingdom, with Your great goodness that You had given them, and in the spacious and fertile land that You had set before them, they did not serve You, nor did they turn from their evil deeds.
Behold, today we are slaves, in the land that You gave to our fathers to eat its fruit and its goodness — behold, we are slaves upon it.
Its abundant produce goes to the kings whom You have set over us because of our sins. They rule over our bodies and over our livestock as they please. We are in great distress.
